scope: Close S-10 by binding the tools service runtime to AgenC-owned root tools modules, including orchestration, execution, streaming dispatch, and hooks.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow upstream source root path

approvedDivergence.reason: The checklist preserves the source service path, while the AgenC architecture and feature matrix designate runtime/src/tools/ as the live owner. S-10 intentionally binds the live phase to runtime/src/tools/ and rejects recreation of the legacy services directory.

requiredBehaviors: Partition consecutive concurrency-safe tool-use blocks into one safe batch.
Treat unknown tools, invalid input, absent safety hooks, and throwing safety hooks as non-concurrency-safe.
Run safe batches concurrently while deferring context modifiers until the batch drains.
Run non-concurrency-safe blocks serially and apply context modifiers immediately.
Use AGENC_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY with a default of 10 and fallback for invalid values.
edgeCases: Safe batch result yield order follows tool-use submission order.
In-progress tool-use IDs are added before dispatch and removed after terminal result yield.
A cap of 1 serializes otherwise safe work without changing result order.

requiredBehaviors: Queue streamed tool calls and dispatch eligible safe calls without waiting for the full model response.
Preserve exclusive/head-of-line blocking for non-concurrency-safe calls.
Yield terminal results in submission order while progress can be interleaved.
Propagate Bash sibling aborts and external abort reasons as terminal tool results.
Honor the global maxConcurrency cap in addition to per-tool concurrency classes.
edgeCases: Unknown tools synthesize a terminal error result without dispatch.
discard() makes all result iterators early-return without synthesizing fallback results.
maxConcurrency does not bypass exclusive tool ordering or per-server runtime locks.

requiredBehaviors: Parse and validate tool arguments before dispatch.
Run pre-tool hooks before approval and dispatch.
Honor permission deny/ask/allow outcomes and updated arguments.
Run post-tool and failure hooks around tool execution.
Preserve progress events, timeout handling, abort handling, and result-size caps.
edgeCases: Invalid JSON arguments produce tool errors without dispatch.
Hook-thrown errors are surfaced through hook diagnostics without skipping the terminal tool result.

requiredBehaviors: Support pre-tool, post-tool, failure, and permission decision hooks.
Merge hook-provided additional context into the execution result path.
Preserve permission hook precedence so hook allow does not bypass rule deny/ask decisions.
Record hook timing and hook-error diagnostics for callers.
edgeCases: Hook result rewrites preserve tool error status.
Permission hook exceptions become diagnostics instead of implicit allow decisions.
